title: Tooth Decay as Depth Invasion

analysis.object: T597: Tooth Decay as Depth Invasion depth analysis

enamel_eml0.reason: Enamel is EML-0: hardest biological material; crystalline, discrete mineral lattice

bacterial_acid_eml1.reason: S. mutans metabolism is EML-1: exponential lactic acid production from sugars

ph_eml2.description: pH measurement determines demineralization threshold: EML-2
ph_eml2.reason: Tooth demineralization is EML-2: critical pH 5.5 threshold; below -> EML-0 crystal dissolution

pain_eml3.description: Nerve pain oscillates as dentine exposed and retreats: EML-3
pain_eml3.reason: Dental pain is EML-3: oscillatory hot/cold sensitivity as nerve exposure increases

depth_invasion.description: Cavity = depth invasion: bacteria force EML-0 crystal -> EML-3 pain via EML-1 acid
depth_invasion.reason: Tooth decay theorem: bacterial EML-1 forces depth traversal 0->1->2->3 in passive crystal
